$description: Composition patterns for the search experience. Search is the AI librarian's interface (DES-003). Pure hybrid retrieval — no AI in the search path. The search experience should feel curated, not algorithmic.

search-input.$description: The search bar. The seeker's primary question to the librarian.

behavior.clear-button: appears when input has value, 44px touch target

states.loading: subtle gold pulse on border (not a spinner)

suggestions-dropdown.$description: Typeahead suggestions. Corpus-derived, not behavior-derived (ADR-049). Static JSON at CDN edge + pg_trgm fuzzy fallback.

tiers.A: Prefix matches from static suggestion list (CDN edge, instant)
tiers.B: pg_trgm fuzzy matches (async, fires when Tier A < 3 results)

search-results.$description: Search results page. Each result is a passage card with the search query highlighted.
search-results.register: sacred for passage text, functional for chrome

layout.max-width: 48em — wider than reader (42em) to accommodate metadata

additions.highlight: Search terms highlighted with gold subliminal (0.06) background + subtle gold border. Not bold — highlighting is background-based, not weight-based.
additions.snippet-context: Surrounding text shown at secondary opacity (0.7)
additions.metadata: Book title, chapter, language badge — ui typography, text-secondary

pagination.ui: 'Load more' button rather than page numbers. No infinite scroll.
pagination.position: centered below results, generous top margin

empty-state.$description: When search returns no results. Honest absence as invitation.
empty-state.message: Gentle, constructive. 'No passages found for [query]. Try [suggestions].'
empty-state.suggestions: Related search terms from vocabulary bridge (DES-059)
empty-state.never: Never say 'No results' without guidance. Never leave the seeker at a dead end.

search-filters.$description: Optional filters for narrowing search. Unobtrusive — visible but not dominant.
search-filters.behavior: Filters are additive. Default is 'all'. Changing a filter re-runs the search.
